title: Gynaecomastia secondary to transdermal oestradiol used for androgen deprivation therapy: informed reality vs subjective fear.
abstract: To provide information that can aid patients with prostate cancer (PCa) and their uro-oncologists in shared decision-making discussions about androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), with transdermal oestradiol (tE2) as one option. We do so by summarising recent literature on breast growth in genetic males undergoing treatment with exogenous E2. Based on the amount of breast development observed in this population, we estimate the maximum amount of breast growth that patients with PCa can expect, if they elect tE2 for ADT. We also present data on risks and management of mastalgia associated with tE2.
